meta.title: Cocora Valley | Quindío's Natural Landscape, Colombia
meta.description: Explore Cocora Valley, a natural wonder in Quindío, Colombia, home to the world's tallest palm trees and breathtaking cloud forest landscapes.

hero.title: Cocora Valley
hero.subtitle: Colombia's Green Gem, Home of the Tallest Palm Trees
hero.hours: National Park / Natural Landscape / Hiking Paradise

intro.title: Explore Cocora Valley
intro.description:
Cocora Valley (Valle de Cocora) is a natural treasure in Quindío, Colombia, located 11 kilometers north of Salento. It is famous for the world's tallest palm trees—the Wax Palm (Palma de Cera), which can grow up to 60 meters high.

As part of Los Nevados National Natural Park, Cocora Valley offers breathtaking cloud forest landscapes, rich biodiversity, and multiple hiking trails. It is one of Colombia's most iconic natural landscapes and part of the Coffee Cultural Landscape UNESCO World Heritage site.

visitGuide.title: Visiting Tips
items: 🌞 Morning Hike Tour: Depart from Salento ➔ Horseback riding or hiking into the valley ➔ Visit the Wax Palm forest
🌙 Afternoon Cultural Tour: Return to Salento ➔ Visit a coffee farm ➔ Taste local cuisine
🎉 Eco Experience: If possible, visit during the dry season (December to March) for better hiking weather

alsoKnownAs.title: Highlights
items: 🌴 World's Tallest Palm Trees: Wax Palms (Palma de Cera) can reach up to 60 meters, Colombia's national tree.
🏞️ Cloud Forest Landscape: Breathtaking Andes cloud forest and river landscapes.
🐎 Hiking and Horseback Riding: Multiple trails of varying difficulty, or choose horseback riding tours.
☕ Coffee Cultural Region: Located within the UNESCO Coffee Cultural Landscape World Heritage area.

route.title: Tour Route
route.overview: The ideal route to explore Cocora Valley:
steps: Take a Willys Jeep from Salento to the Cocora Valley entrance.
Start hiking or horseback riding along the marked trails to the Wax Palm viewpoint.
Enjoy a picnic in the valley while admiring the surrounding natural landscapes.
Return to Salento and visit local coffee farms or taste traditional cuisine.
supplements: Wear comfortable hiking shoes and bring rain gear (valley weather is changeable).
Entrance fee is approximately COP $10,000-15,000, subject to change.
Willys Jeeps from Salento typically start at 6:00 AM, with the last return around 5:00 PM.

knowledge.title: Discover the Charm of Cocora Valley

sections.title: History & Conservation
sections.content: Cocora Valley is part of Los Nevados National Natural Park. The Wax Palm is Colombia's national tree and is protected by law. It is also an important part of the UNESCO Coffee Cultural Landscape World Heritage site.

sections.title: Biodiversity
sections.content: The valley hosts rich biodiversity, including various bird species (such as macaws and hummingbirds), orchids, and other flora and fauna endemic to the Andes.

sections.title: Hiking Paradise
sections.content: There are multiple hiking trails here, from easy family trails to challenging multi-day treks, suitable for visitors of all levels.

basicInfo.title: Quick Facts
basicInfo.typeValue: National Park / Natural Landscape / Hiking Paradise
basicInfo.addressValue: Vía al Valle de Cocora, Km 11, Salento, Quindío, Colombia

hours.title: Opening Hours & Visitor Info
hours.winterTime: Wax Palm forest, cloud forest, river landscapes
hours.warningTime: Arrive early in the morning to avoid crowds and see the most beautiful morning mist. Remember to bring sunscreen and rain gear!
hours.tip: From the viewpoint, you can see the Wax Palms emerging mysteriously from the morning mist.

tickets.title: Important Information
tickets.cardPrice: Salento town, coffee farms. 👉 [Go to Cocora Valley]
tickets.officialNotice: For the latest updates on entrance fees and opening hours, please refer to the
tickets.officialNoticeLinkLabel: Official Website of Colombia's National Natural Parks

transport.title: Transportation
transport.airportDesc: From the nearest airports (Pereira or Armenia), you need to first reach Salento town, then take a Willys Jeep to Cocora Valley.
transport.selfDriveDesc: From Salento, follow the clearly marked road for about 11 kilometers to reach the valley entrance.
transport.busDesc: Taking a Willys Jeep from Salento town center is the most popular transportation option, with frequent departures.
transport.intercityDesc: The valley has clearly marked hiking trails suitable for hikers of all levels.
transport.cyclingDesc: You can choose horseback riding tours with local guide services available.
transport.tipsDesc: Willys Jeeps typically start operating at 6:00 AM. It's recommended to depart early to avoid crowds.

gallery.title: Stunning Photos
gallery.subtitle: Feel Colombia's Most Magnificent Natural Landscape
gallery.viewAll: View more photos on Google Maps

reviews.title: Visitor Reviews
reviews.declaration: Review information can be viewed via Google Maps (external link).
reviews.moreReviews: View More Reviews on Google Maps

items.name: Carlos Mendoza
items.text: One of the most beautiful hiking spots in the world! The Wax Palms tower into the clouds, the scenery is breathtaking.

items.name: Li Xiaoming
items.text: Taking the Willys Jeep from Salento to get here is so much fun. The hiking trails are clear, the scenery is stunning, highly recommended!

items.name: María González
items.text: A must-visit place in Colombia! The morning mist makes the entire valley look like a fairyland.

items.name: John Smith
items.text: An unforgettable experience! I chose horseback riding, and the local guides were very friendly and professional.

items.name: Ana Silva
items.text: Beautiful scenery, but it gets quite crowded on weekends. Recommend visiting on weekdays for a better experience in nature.

items.name: Zhang Wei
items.text: Absolutely worth a visit! Remember to bring your camera, every angle is a postcard-perfect view.

items.name: Roberto Pérez
items.text: This is Colombia's pride! The Wax Palm is the national tree, and seeing them so tall is truly awe-inspiring.

items.name: Emma Wilson
items.text: I completed the full hiking trail, which took about 6 hours. Tiring but absolutely worth it!

historyTimeline.title: Historical Timeline

items.title: Early Period: Nature Conservation & Discovery
items.description: Cocora Valley, as part of Los Nevados National Natural Park, has long been an important nature reserve and biodiversity hotspot in Colombia.

items.title: 20th Century: Eco-Tourism Development
items.description: With the growth of tourism in Colombia, Cocora Valley gradually became one of the most popular eco-tourism destinations, attracting visitors from around the world.

items.title: 21st Century: World Heritage Recognition
items.description: As part of the Colombian Coffee Cultural Landscape, the region was inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List, gaining international recognition and protection.

historyTimeline.guideContent: When visiting, you can take a Willys Jeep from Salento to Cocora Valley; then follow the hiking trails to admire the Wax Palm forest and cloud forest; if you have enough time, you can hike or horseback ride in the valley to experience Colombia's natural beauty. Finally, return to Salento to explore the charm of the town.

officialManagement.title: About Cocora Valley
officialManagement.text: Cocora Valley is a famous natural landscape in Quindío, Colombia, known for the world's tallest palm trees—the Wax Palm. It is managed by Colombia's National Natural Parks administration and relevant local governments.
